skill.name: multi-agent-brainstorming
skill.description: Structured design review workflow that assigns separate agent roles for design, challenge, constraints, user advocacy, and arbitration.
skill.summary: Run sequential multi-agent design reviews with explicit roles, objections, decisions, and exit criteria.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external commands, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance terms, but review found only Markdown prose and inline code formatting. No executable scripts, network access, filesystem access, credential handling, or prompt injection attempts were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Skill Reference Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The two external command detections are Markdown references to the brainstorming skill. They do not execute Ruby, shell, or any external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are instructional Markdown bullets with inline code formatting. No shell syntax, script block, or execution mechanism is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matched Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections appear to match ordinary documentation text about design review. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, or implementation code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is documentation-only Markdown about design review roles and process. No weak cryptographic primitive or cryptography-related behavior appears at the cited locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Pattern Matched Documentation
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance detections occur in plain documentation about design validity and failure review. They do not invoke commands or inspect the host system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural-language Markdown sentences. There is no command syntax, subprocess call, or request for system information.

content.value_statement: Single-agent design reviews can miss hidden assumptions and risks. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through sequenced reviewer roles, decision logs, and exit criteria.
actual_capabilities: Defines separate roles for designer, challenger, constraint reviewer, user advocate, and arbiter.
Runs reviews sequentially to reduce duplicated feedback and role confusion.
Requires objections to reference assumptions, decisions, or constraints.
Maintains a decision log for alternatives, objections, resolutions, and rationale.
Uses explicit exit criteria before a design can move to implementation.
limitations: It provides a review process, not a separate autonomous agent runtime.
It depends on the assistant having access to the referenced brainstorming skill.
It does not validate facts outside the information supplied by the user.
It can slow small decisions that do not need formal review.

use_cases.title: Review Product Designs
use_cases.description: Use structured reviewer roles before committing to a product flow or feature direction.

use_cases.title: Stress-Test Technical Architecture
use_cases.description: Identify reliability, security, scalability, and maintainability concerns before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Improve Decision Quality
use_cases.description: Create a clear record of assumptions, objections, accepted changes, and rejected alternatives.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Design Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Start a lightweight review for a proposed idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use multi-agent brainstorming to review this idea: [describe the idea]. Keep the review focused on risks, constraints, and user impact.

prompt_templates.title: Feature Planning Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluate a feature before planning implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a structured multi-agent review for this feature proposal: [feature]. Include assumptions, reviewer objections, revisions, and a decision log.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Risk Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Assess a technical design before engineering work begins.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the full review process on this architecture: [architecture]. Focus on failure modes, security, reliability, operational cost, and exit criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Formal Arbitration Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Resolve conflicting feedback and decide whether to proceed.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply multi-agent brainstorming to this design and produce a final disposition of APPROVED, REVISE, or REJECT with rationale.

output_examples.input: Review a new onboarding flow for a business software product.
output_examples.output: The review identifies unclear first-run defaults, missing error recovery, and a decision to revise the setup flow before implementation.

output_examples.input: Evaluate an event processing architecture before build starts.
output: The challenger flags retry loops and hidden ordering assumptions.
The constraint reviewer requests explicit throughput targets.
The arbiter marks the design REVISE until limits are documented.

output_examples.input: Assess whether a proposed workflow is ready for implementation.
output_examples.output: The final disposition is APPROVED after all objections are resolved and the decision log records accepted tradeoffs.

best_practices: Provide clear requirements, constraints, and success criteria before starting the review.
Keep each reviewer limited to its assigned role and scope.
Treat unresolved objections as blockers until the arbiter records a decision.
anti_patterns: Using the process for trivial decisions that need a quick answer.
Allowing reviewers to add new requirements after the scope is locked.
Proceeding to implementation before the decision log and exit criteria are complete.

faq.question: Does this skill run multiple agents in parallel?
faq.answer: No. It defines a sequential review process with separate roles and gated decisions.

faq.question: What is the main output?
faq.answer: The main output is a reviewed design with objections, revisions, a decision log, and a final disposition.

faq.question: Can it replace human review?
faq.answer: No. It improves preparation and structure, but human owners should approve important decisions.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when a design has meaningful risk, unclear assumptions, or high implementation cost.

faq.question: Does it create implementation code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed to review ideas and designs before implementation begins.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
